response.justification: Maria brings genuine breadth across distributed systems, TypeScript, and — critically — current hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq, which directly maps to the role's AI-native engineering baseline. However, Python is entirely absent from the resume despite being a stated must-have, and the distributed-systems depth at scale is concentrated in a single 22-month role at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic. A short screening call focused specifically on Python proficiency and operational ownership history would clarify whether these are resume omissions or actual skill gaps before investing further.
strengths: Active AI/agentic engineering experience: current role at re:cinq involves MCP code generators and agentic-workflow frameworks, directly matching the AI-native environment expectation
Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems work at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency microservices and async I/O
Broad polyglot background with Staff-level titles at multiple companies and consistent evidence of TDD discipline, CI/CD ownership, and software craftsmanship
concerns: Python is completely absent from the resume — not listed in skills, projects, or any employment history — despite being a must-have for this role

Distributed systems depth appears concentrated in a single 22-month role; most other positions are product or tooling engineering, which may not meet the Staff-level bar for architecture-at-scale decisions
No explicit evidence of cross-functional stakeholder management, on-call incident response, or technical strategy ownership beyond a very recent and short-tenure Staff role at re:cinq
